codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the bug by setting worktree.ready = false after cleanup_worktree() removes the physical worktree. This ensures is_available() returns false for deleted worktrees since it checks both agent_id.is_none() and ready.

summary: The fix is correct and minimal. In the release() method, after cleanup_worktree() removes the physical worktree, the code now explicitly sets worktree.ready = false. This synchronizes the ready flag with the actual worktree existence, fixing is_available() to return false for deleted worktrees. Build succeeds and all 9 worktree tests pass. Worker followed the Rust skill procedure correctly.
